title: Add support for Simplified Layer normalization in the EP
description: It is not clear what exactly qualifies as Simplified Layer Normalization, but find out what computation this is in ONNX Runtime and support it through the EP

comments.author: MaheshRavishankar
comments.text:
Research complete: SimplifiedLayerNormalization is RMS Norm (Y = X * Scale / sqrt(mean(X^2) + epsilon)) — no mean subtraction, no bias. It is a com.microsoft contrib op in ORT, registered in kOnnxDomain. Inputs: X, Scale. Outputs: Y, optional inv_std_var. Attributes: axis, epsilon, stash_type.

hipDNN frontend HAS native RMSNormNode (Graph::rmsnorm(x, scale, attrs)) with RMSNormAttributes supporting inference and training phases.

However, the EP's backend engine (FUSILLI_ENGINE) does NOT support RMSNorm — fusilli only has LayerNormNode (standard layer norm with mean subtraction). Decomposition into supported fusilli ops also fails because RSQRT/SQRT are commented out in fusilli's pointwise modes.

Blocked until fusilli adds one of: (1) native RMSNorm node support, or (2) RSQRT/SQRT pointwise ops (enabling decomposition).

title: Support channels-last (NHWC) convolutions in the EP
description: Add NHWC layout support for convolutions. Currently the EP only produces NCHW strides. ReshapeBiasForConv assumes NCHW and needs to handle NHWC bias reshaping ([1,1,1,C] instead of [1,C,1,1]). The conv node builders and IsSupportedConv should also handle NHWC layout.

title: Add support for reduction operations in the EP
description: Primary goal is to support sum reductions, but it should be possible to support any reduction that is already supported by hipDNN. Implementation MUST use the hipDNN API (not custom HIP kernels). Check hipDNN headers for reduction tensor operations (e.g. hipdnnReduceTensor or equivalent in the Fusilli/graph API).

comments.author: MaheshRavishankar
comments.text: Implementation complete. All tests pass (non-MLIR: 6 reduction + existing, MLIR: all pass). Ready for review.

comments.author: MaheshRavishankar
comments.text: Research complete: hipDNN frontend Graph API does not expose a reduction() method. Only Fusilli's internal Graph class has graph.reduction(). The EP links against hipdnn_frontend (dist), not Fusilli directly. Blocked until hipDNN frontend exposes the reduction API from Fusilli.

title: Allow hipDNN to select engine per-op instead of forcing Fusilli for all operations
description: Currently the EP forces FUSILLI_ENGINE for the entire graph (hipdnn_graph.cc line 1123: graph_->set_preferred_engine_id_ext("FUSILLI_ENGINE")). This blocks operations that hipDNN frontend supports but Fusilli doesn't (e.g. reduction, RMS norm). Change the engine selection so that Fusilli is preferred for conv/gemm/pointwise but other ops let hipDNN pick the best available engine. This may require per-node engine hints or removing the global engine override and letting the backend choose.

comments.author: MaheshRavishankar
comments.text: Clarification: Fusilli should still be preferred for conv/gemm/pointwise graphs. Only skip it when the graph contains ops that Fusilli doesn't support (e.g. reduction, RMS norm). The fix should conditionally set the engine, not remove it entirely.
